name: Penza Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the fields and forests of Penza
the icon's depiction of the Saviour

description: A green vertical stripe along the hoist (about 3/16 of the length) and a golden-yellow main field; the icon of the Saviour Not Made by Hands is centred on the golden field.
history: Adopted on 28 December 2002 by the Legislative Assembly of Penza Oblast; the central image reproduces the icon carried on the historic Penza standard of 1717.

features.symbolism: the green stripe represents the fertile fields and forests of the Penza land; the wide golden field stands for wisdom, light and prosperity.
the icon of the Saviour Not Made by Hands (Spas Nerukotvorny), patron of the Penza land, taken from the historic Penza standard.
